content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 24, 1992


Jerry Lee Wilson
P. O. Box 278
Patton, CA  92369

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-92-390

Dear Mr. Wilson:

     This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
and confirms the telephone advice provided to you regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

                              QUESTIONS

     1.  May the Committee to Recall Eaves for Freedom in America
make a contribution of campaign funds to a candidate controlled
committee?

     2.  May the Committee to Recall Eaves for Freedom in America
discard items which have no cash value and for which the committee
has been unable to locate a purchaser?

                            CONCLUSIONS

     1.  The committee may make a contribution of campaign funds
to a candidate controlled committee if the contribution is
reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose of the committee.

     2.  The committee may discard items which have no cash value
and for which the committee has been unable to locate a buyer.

                              FACTS

     You are the treasurer for the Committee to Recall Eaves for
Freedom in America, #890868.  Your recall efforts have failed and

______________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95812  •  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A.92-390
Page 2


you wish to terminate the committee.  The committee has remaining
funds of approximately $525.  The committee also has an inventory
of items which were donated to the committee but which have no
cash value and for which you have been unable to locate a buyer.

     The committee wishes to contribute its remaining funds to a
candidate seeking the seat now held by Mr. Eaves in the 66th
Assembly District.

                              ANALYSIS

     Under the provisions of the Act, an expenditure of campaign
funds must be reasonably related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.  (Section 89512.)  A contribution made to a
candidate would appear to be political in nature.  Accordingly,
your committee may contribute its remaining funds to a candidate
seeking election to the office now occupied by the individual your
committee attempted to recall.

     There are no provisions in the Act which would prevent a
committee from discarding inventory which has no value and for
which the committee cannot locate a purchaser.  However, because
inventory is in the nature of assets of the committee, upon
closing the committee you must account for this inventory.  It
would be advisable to compile a list of the items the committee
will discard indicating that the items have no value.  In
addition, disposition of these items may not confer a substantial
personal benefit on controlling members of the committee such as
yourself.  A "substantial personal benefit" is defined as a direct
personal benefit of $100 or more.  (Section 89511(b)(2).)

     We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.2

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]

                                        By:  Blanca M. Breeze
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:bb


___________________________
     2  Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
